pi_items.source: Web search confirmed Chimney Rock National Monument in Colorado is a famous site for observing the Major Lunar Standstill.
Web search confirms Chimney Rock Great House coordinates are approximately 37.192 N, matching the prompts intended 37.193.
Web search confirms Chimney Rock coordinates are approximately -107.306 W, matching the prompts intended -107.309 closely.
Python math.radians(37.193) evaluates to ~0.64914 rad, precisely matching the verifier scripts target.

Python math.radians(-107.309) equals ~ -1.872895 rad. The claimed value -1.87288 rad mathematically equates to -107.3081 degrees, showing a slight precision contradiction in the tasks stated ground truth (though the verifiers 0.05 rad tolerance allows it to pass).
The prompt explicitly instructs the user to configure the altitude to 2300 m. Web search confirms the real-world elevation is ~2316m (7600 ft).
Web search verified that the northern extreme moonrise for the 18.6-year Major Lunar Standstill cycle visibly occurred at Chimney Rock on December 15, 2024.
Web search verified moonrise was ~4:38 PM MST (23:38 UTC), making 23:50 UTC an appropriate observational time for the event simulation.
Code analysis of verifier.py confirms these exact boolean states are strictly evaluated for maximum score.
Code analysis of export_result.sh and verifier.py confirms this specific file path is parsed and checked for content.
Code analysis of export_result.sh confirms screenshots are counted in this exact directory.
Code analysis of verifier.py confirms full screenshot points (15 pts) are awarded when new_screenshot_count >= 1.

privileged_info_summary: The task requires configuring Stellarium for the Major Lunar Standstill at Chimney Rock Great House on December 15, 2024. Web searches confirm this date hosted the northernmost moonrise of the 18.6-year cycle, visible around 4:38 PM MST (23:38 UTC), aligning perfectly with the requested 23:50:00 UTC observation time. The task instructions mandate latitude 37.193 N and longitude 107.309 W; while the verifier uses a target_lon_rad of -1.87288 (which mathematically converts to -107.3081 degrees instead of -107.309 degrees), the verifiers broad 0.05 rad tolerance easily accommodates this minor discrepancy. Code analysis of verifier.py and export_result.sh confirms the evaluation strictly checks for the precise display flags (atmosphere off, landscape on, azimuthal grid on, cardinal points on), a screenshot saved to /home/ga/Pictures/stellarium, and an educational brief written to /home/ga/Desktop/standstill_brief.txt.
